_meta.description: App-wide read-only seed: LinkedIn post-body structural template library. body_templates = 77 single-line structural patterns (4 categories) for hook+framework composition; full_post_templates = 5 complete verbatim post skeletons (A-E) with char counts and use cases.
_meta.extraction_notes: Source doc titled '70+ / 74 body templates'. The actual numbered table rows total 77 (Actionable 35 + Analytical 15 + Motivational 22 + Story 5). All 77 emitted verbatim — the '74' is the doc's stated count, the rows are the real data. category_counts_stated_in_source preserved per Negative-Proof discipline (the prose subheads say ~35/~15/~20/~5; the Motivational table actually has 22 numbered rows including two rows both labeled 'What they say / what they know' at #5 and #22, both preserved verbatim).
_meta.how_to_use_pipeline: Pick a pattern that fits the topic -> pair with a hook from the hooks library -> apply a copywriting framework (AIDA, PAS, BAB, etc.) for full structure -> write using the Writing Helper 4-step process.

full_post_templates.name: The Comprehensive Breakdown
full_post_templates.when_to_use: Tech stack reveals, company deep dives, system architectures
full_post_templates.skeleton:
[HOOK with specific numbers]

[1-2 line credibility builder]

[Brief preview of what's coming]

1️⃣ [SECTION NAME]

[Brief intro]

We use:
- Tool A (specific use case)
- Tool B (specific use case)
- Tool C (specific use case)

2️⃣ [SECTION NAME]

[Context or insight]

For [specific outcome]:
→ Action with detail
→ Action with detail
→ Action with detail

[Continue for 5-7 sections]

P.S: [Engagement question]

full_post_templates.name: The Listicle Framework
full_post_templates.when_to_use: Lessons learned, principles, frameworks, hiring advice
full_post_templates.skeleton:
[HOOK with journey/result]

Here are X things I learned:

1. [Principle Name]

[2-3 sentence explanation]

→ Actionable sub-point
→ Actionable sub-point
→ Actionable sub-point

2. [Principle Name]

[Pattern explanation]

One way I do this:
→ Action step
→ Action step

[Continue for 5-8 principles]

P.S: [Question about their situation]

full_post_templates.name: The Case Study / Before-After
full_post_templates.when_to_use: Client results (anonymized), campaign breakdowns, ROI stories
full_post_templates.skeleton:
[HOOK: Bold result + metric]

[Context: What the situation was BEFORE]

Here's what was broken:
→ Problem 1 (with number)
→ Problem 2 (with number)
→ Problem 3 (with number)

Here's what we changed:
→ Fix 1 (specific action + tool)
→ Fix 2 (specific action + tool)
→ Fix 3 (specific action + tool)

The results after [timeframe]:
→ Metric 1 (before → after)
→ Metric 2 (before → after)
→ Metric 3 (before → after)

TAKEAWAY:

[1-2 sentence principle]

[Follow-up question or CTA]

full_post_templates.name: The Comment-Gated Lead Magnet
full_post_templates.when_to_use: Maximum engagement, list building, lead magnet distribution
full_post_templates.why_it_works: Comment-gating drives 3-5x normal comment volume. The algorithm sees massive comment count → boosts distribution. Best-performing posts on ColdIQ accounts consistently use this template.
full_post_templates.skeleton:
[HOOK: Bold claim with specific number]

[2-3 lines explaining WHY this matters]

[Brief preview of what you'll get — 3-5 bullet teaser]

→ Teaser point 1
→ Teaser point 2
→ Teaser point 3

[The "But here's the BONUS" escalation]

[Final CTA: Comment [KEYWORD] and I'll share it]

full_post_templates.name: The Conversational Story
full_post_templates.when_to_use: Leadership, agency scaling, relationship content, personal takes
full_post_templates.skeleton:
[HOOK: Punchy statement or provocative claim]

[Short personal story or observation — conversational, no fluff]

Here's the thing:

[Core insight in 2-3 sentences]

[Supporting points — short, punchy]
- Point 1
- Point 2
- Point 3

[Reflection or bigger-picture takeaway]

[Close with personal conviction, not a CTA]
